description: clawverify.com (Verification API) — PRD — Universal signature verifier for artifacts, messages, receipts, and attestations. Fail-closed on unknown schema/versions.

userStories.title: Verify artifact signatures
userStories.description: As a verifier, I want to validate artifact envelopes so that I can prove authorship.
acceptanceCriteria: Reject unknown version/type/algo
Recompute hash and match envelope
Return VALID/INVALID with reason
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented Cloudflare Worker with fail-closed validation and Ed25519 verification

userStories.title: Verify message signatures
userStories.description: As a platform, I want to validate signed messages so that I can bind DIDs to accounts.
acceptanceCriteria: Support message_signature envelopes
Fail if signature invalid
Return signer DID
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented /v1/verify/message endpoint with account_binding, ownership_proof, and challenge_response message types

userStories.title: Verify gateway receipts
userStories.description: As a marketplace, I want to validate proxy receipts so that I can enforce proof-of-harness.
acceptanceCriteria: Validate receipt signature
Check receipt schema
Return verified provider/model
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented /v1/verify/receipt endpoint with GatewayReceiptPayload validation and provider/model return

userStories.title: Batch verification
userStories.description: As a auditor, I want to submit multiple envelopes so that I can verify at scale.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /v1/verify/batch
Return per-item results
Limit batch size to prevent abuse
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented /v1/verify/batch endpoint with 100 item limit, auto-detects envelope type, returns per-item results with valid/invalid counts

userStories.title: Verification provenance
userStories.description: As a compliance officer, I want verification results logged so that audits are traceable.
acceptanceCriteria: Write hash-chained audit log entry
Include request hash + timestamp
Allow retrieval by receipt id
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented D1-backed audit log with hash chaining, /v1/provenance/{receipt_id} retrieval endpoint, and chain verification

userStories.title: Public docs and schema registry
userStories.description: As a developer, I want clear verification docs so that I can integrate quickly.
acceptanceCriteria: Publish schema versions
Provide example payloads
Include fail-closed rules
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented /v1/schemas and /v1/schemas/{id} endpoints with schema definitions, examples, and fail-closed rules for all 8 envelope types

userStories.title: Verify proof bundles
userStories.description: As a marketplace, I want proof bundle verification so that trust tiers are automated.
acceptanceCriteria: Validate URM + event chain + receipts + attestations
Fail closed on unknown schema/version
Return computed trust tier
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented /v1/verify/bundle endpoint with URM, event chain, receipts, and attestations validation. Trust tier computation (unknown/basic/verified/attested/full) based on validated components.

userStories.title: Verify event chains
userStories.description: As a auditor, I want event chain verification so that logs are tamper-evident.
acceptanceCriteria: Validate hash chain and root
Enforce run_id consistency
Return chain_root_hash and error codes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented /v1/verify/event-chain endpoint with hash chain integrity validation, run_id consistency checks, chain_root_hash return, and dedicated error codes (EMPTY_CHAIN, INCONSISTENT_RUN_ID, HASH_CHAIN_BREAK, INVALID_ROOT)

userStories.title: Schema registry allowlist
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a schema registry so that validation is deterministic.
acceptanceCriteria: Publish allowlisted schema ids + versions
Reject unknown ids by default
Provide example payloads per schema
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented SCHEMA_ALLOWLIST with 8 schema IDs and versions. Added GET /v1/schemas/allowlist (list with examples), POST /v1/schemas/validate (fail-closed validation), GET /v1/schemas/:id/example endpoints. Unknown schema IDs return 422 with error details.

userStories.title: Verify owner attestations
userStories.description: As a platform, I want owner verification so that sybil resistance is possible.
acceptanceCriteria: Validate owner attestation envelope
Check expiry and provider reference
Return verified/expired/unknown status
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented /v1/verify/owner-attestation endpoint with fail-closed owner_attestation envelope validation, expiry handling (EXPIRED), and owner_status (verified/expired/unknown) semantics.

userStories.title: Verify commit proofs
userStories.description: As a reviewer, I want commit proof verification so that agent work is trusted.
acceptanceCriteria: Validate commit proof envelope
Ensure repo claim exists in clawclaim
Return repo + commit + signer DID
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented /v1/verify/commit-proof endpoint with fail-closed commit_proof envelope validation, repo_claim_id allowlist check (clawclaim registry), and response includes repository + commit_sha + signer_did.

userStories.title: One-call agent verification
userStories.description: As a platform, I want a single verify call so that integrations are easy.
acceptanceCriteria: Return DID validity + owner status + PoH tier
Include policy compliance (if WPC present)
Include risk flags (optional)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added POST /v1/verify/agent endpoint aggregating DID validity, owner attestation status, and PoH tier (mapped from proof bundle trust_tier). Optional policy_hash support returns policy_compliance when present and includes risk_flags + component results; verification fails closed (422) when provided components fail or mismatched subjects.

userStories.title: Scoped token introspection
userStories.description: As a service, I want token introspection so that authorization is safe.
acceptanceCriteria: Validate token signature + expiry
Return scope + audience + owner_ref
Log token hash to clawlogs
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added POST /v1/introspect/scoped-token: validates scoped_token envelope signature + expires_at, returns scope/audience/owner_ref and token_hash_b64u. Logs token hash via structured console log and anchors audit log request_hash_b64u to token_hash_b64u for correlation.

userStories.title: Public landing + skill docs
userStories.description: As a developer, I want public landing/docs/skill endpoints so that I can discover and integrate clawverify quickly.
acceptanceCriteria: GET / returns an HTML landing page with links to /docs and /skill.md
GET /docs returns minimal human-readable docs (HTML or markdown)
GET /skill.md returns an OpenClaw-compatible skill file (frontmatter + markdown)
OpenClaw frontmatter: metadata is a single-line JSON object string
GET /robots.txt and /sitemap.xml exist (minimal)
GET /.well-known/security.txt exists
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added public endpoints to clawverify worker: GET /, /docs, /skill.md (OpenClaw frontmatter with metadata as single-line JSON object string), /robots.txt, /sitemap.xml, and /.well-known/security.txt.

userStories.title: Golden vector crypto correctness gate
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want an authoritative golden vector test so did:key derivation, JCS canonicalization, and Ed25519 signature verification remain compatible across implementations.
acceptanceCriteria: Load the protocol-m golden vector fixture from packages/schema/fixtures
Verify did:key extraction yields the expected public key
Verify RFC 8785-style JCS canonicalization yields the expected canonical string
Verify Ed25519 signature over the canonical UTF-8 bytes
Test suite passes
userStories.notes: Added vitest coverage using packages/schema/fixtures/protocol-m-golden-vector.v1.json. Test validates did:key extraction, JCS canonicalization, and Ed25519 signature verification, plus a tamper-negative case.

userStories.title: Verify DID rotation certificates
userStories.description: As a platform, I want to verify dual-signed DID rotation certificates so trust can survive key rotation without weakening the threat model.
acceptanceCriteria: Add POST /v1/verify/did-rotation endpoint
Verify both old and new did:key Ed25519 signatures over RFC 8785 JCS canonical bytes (signatures blanked during canonicalization)
Fail closed on unknown fields and malformed certificates
Test suite passes
userStories.notes: Implemented did_rotation certificate verification (dual signatures) with a small RFC 8785 JCS canonicalizer, plus vitest coverage for valid/tampered/unknown-field cases. Added endpoint to docs and /skill.md metadata.

userStories.title: Rotation-aware /v1/verify/agent
userStories.description: As a platform, I want /v1/verify/agent to accept DID rotation certificates so older proof components can be used after key rotation without lowering security.
acceptanceCriteria: VerifyAgentRequest accepts did_rotation_certificates[]
Fail closed if any provided rotation certificate is invalid
Allow owner attestation subject_did mismatch only when it rotates forward to agent_did
Allow proof bundle agent_did mismatch only when it rotates forward to agent_did
Add tests for rotation and no-rotation mismatch cases
userStories.notes: Added optional did_rotation_certificates to VerifyAgentRequest. verifyAgent now verifies provided rotation certs (fail-closed) and allows subject binding mismatches only when old→new rotation is proven. Added vitest coverage for rotated owner attestations.

userStories.title: Identity control-chain + token-control verification
userStories.description: As an operator, I want deterministic verification of owner/controller/agent chains and token-control claims so sensitive auth decisions are auditable and fail-closed.
acceptanceCriteria: Add one-call owner/controller/agent chain verification endpoint
Add token-control verification endpoint (scope hash, aud/scope, exp, revocation, transition matrix)
Return deterministic remediation hints for invalid outcomes
Typecheck and tests pass
userStories.notes: Implemented `POST /v1/verify/control-chain` and `POST /v1/verify/token-control` in `services/clawverify/src/index.ts` with new modules `verify-control-chain.ts` and `verify-token-control.ts`. Verification is fail-closed with deterministic error codes (`CONTROL_CHAIN_NOT_FOUND`, `CONTROL_CHAIN_CONTEXT_MISMATCH`, `TOKEN_CONTROL_SCOPE_HASH_MISMATCH`, `TOKEN_CONTROL_SCOPE_MISSING`, `TOKEN_CONTROL_AUDIENCE_MISMATCH`, `TOKEN_CONTROL_TRANSITION_FORBIDDEN`, etc.) and deterministic remediation hints. Added tests `test/control-chain.test.ts` and `test/token-control.test.ts`; passed `npm run typecheck && npm test`. Updated deploys: staging `c2602134-d6ad-4110-b71e-2837edf59082`, prod `8d79f1f9-1a97-4b61-a796-0d1622f0181e`; updated smoke artifacts: `artifacts/smoke/identity-control-plane/2026-02-11T22-42-34-634Z-staging/result.json`, `artifacts/smoke/identity-control-plane/2026-02-11T22-42-46-640Z-prod/result.json`.

userStories.title: Deterministic key-interoperability error mapping
userStories.description: As downstream gates, we need token-control verifier errors to map upstream kid failures deterministically for automated remediation.
acceptanceCriteria: Map clawscope `TOKEN_UNKNOWN_KID` to `TOKEN_CONTROL_KEY_UNKNOWN`
Map clawscope `TOKEN_KID_EXPIRED` to `TOKEN_CONTROL_KEY_EXPIRED`
Preserve fail-closed verification behavior
Typecheck and tests pass
userStories.notes: Implemented in `services/clawverify/src/verify-token-control.ts` with new deterministic mapping logic. Added tests in `services/clawverify/test/token-control.test.ts` covering both mappings.

userStories.title: Verifier remediation semantics for key-overlap operations
userStories.description: As operators, we need remediation hints that explicitly point to key-overlap synchronization and reissue flows.
acceptanceCriteria: Key-unknown and key-expired failures include deterministic remediation hints
Hints reference overlap contract/jwks refresh semantics
Typecheck and tests pass
userStories.notes: Added deterministic remediation hints for key overlap failures (`REISSUE_TOKEN`, `SYNC_REVOCATION_STREAM`) in token-control verification responses.

userStories.title: Token-control compatibility contract for downstream integrators
userStories.description: As integration teams, we need a stable compatibility note for kid-overlap transitions and deterministic fail-closed behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Publish compatibility guidance with explicit error semantics
Demonstrate behavior with staging/prod interop smoke evidence
Typecheck/tests/smoke pass
userStories.notes: Published compatibility guidance in `scripts/identity/COMPATIBILITY-NOTE-AGENT-C-token-kid.md` and identity runbook `scripts/identity/RUNBOOK-identity-control-plane-operations.md`; validated cross-environment interop via `scripts/identity/smoke-scope-kid-interop.mjs` artifact `artifacts/smoke/identity-control-plane/2026-02-11T22-43-00-300Z-kid-interop/result.json`.

userStories.title: Token-control transparency freshness and kid governance checks
userStories.description: As downstream policy gates, we need token-control verification to enforce key transparency freshness and accepted-kid membership deterministically.
acceptanceCriteria: Token-control verification validates transparency snapshot age against configured max age
Unknown/expired transparency kid outcomes map to deterministic fail-closed errors
Verification responses include transparency snapshot context for auditability
Typecheck and tests pass
userStories.notes: Implemented in `services/clawverify/src/verify-token-control.ts` with deterministic errors `TOKEN_CONTROL_TRANSPARENCY_STALE`, `TOKEN_CONTROL_TRANSPARENCY_KID_UNKNOWN`, `TOKEN_CONTROL_TRANSPARENCY_KID_EXPIRED` and `transparency_snapshot` response field. Config gate: `CONTROL_KEY_TRANSPARENCY_MAX_AGE_SECONDS`. Validated by M6 suite staging/prod deploy summaries under `artifacts/ops/identity-control-plane/2026-02-12T00-52-18-553Z-staging/` and `.../2026-02-12T00-52-32-525Z-prod/`.

userStories.title: Unify VIR verification core across receipt and proof-bundle paths
userStories.description: As a verifier, I want one shared VIR validation engine so v1/v2 semantics cannot drift between /v1/verify/receipt and /v1/verify/bundle.
acceptanceCriteria: Add src/vir-core.ts with shared VIR validation entrypoint and shared helper functions
verify-receipt.ts and verify-proof-bundle.ts both call the shared VIR core for v1/v2 checks
Shared core enforces legal binding, selective-disclosure merkle recompute, and evidence_conflicts precedence
Duplicate VIR v2 logic is removed from call sites
Typecheck and relevant tests pass
userStories.notes: Implemented shared VIR core in `services/clawverify/src/vir-core.ts` and wired both `verify-receipt.ts` and `verify-proof-bundle.ts` through `validateVirReceiptCore(...)`. Removed duplicate VIR v2 merkle/conflict/binding logic from both call sites. Validated with `npm run typecheck` and VIR suites including `test/vir-core-consistency.test.ts`, `test/vir-v2-receipt.test.ts`, and `test/vir-v2-proof-bundle.test.ts`.

userStories.title: Replace string-matching with structured critical VIR failure codes
userStories.description: As a verifier, I want deterministic fail-closed behavior keyed by explicit error codes rather than brittle message matching.
acceptanceCriteria: Define explicit VIR failure code union/type (e.g. ERR_MERKLE_ROOT_MISMATCH, ERR_CONFLICT_UNREPORTED, ERR_PRECEDENCE_VIOLATION)
Bundle verification critical-gates on structured codes, not error.includes(...)
Critical VIR failures always return INVALID with deterministic EVIDENCE_MISMATCH mapping
Add tests that assert code-based fail-closed behavior
Typecheck and tests pass
userStories.notes: Structured VIR failure codes are now authoritative (`VirFailureCode` in `vir-core.ts`) and proof-bundle critical gating uses `CRITICAL_VIR_CODES` instead of message matching. `verify-proof-bundle.ts` now fail-closes via explicit codes (`ERR_MERKLE_ROOT_MISMATCH`, `ERR_CONFLICT_UNREPORTED`, `ERR_PRECEDENCE_VIOLATION`). Added table-driven cross-path regression coverage in `test/vir-core-consistency.test.ts`.

userStories.title: Deterministic proof-tiering with strict witnessed_web namespace isolation
userStories.description: As a marketplace, I want proof tiers computed deterministically so witnessed web evidence never overclaims gateway trust.
acceptanceCriteria: Proof tier comparator is deterministic (source priority + timestamp + receipt_id tie-break)
web_receipts can uplift only to witnessed_web and never gateway
gateway tier requires gateway receipts or high-confidence VIR sources per policy
sandbox tier uplift remains isolated to attestations/execution attestations
Add/extend tests for tier determinism and namespace isolation
userStories.notes: Proof-tiering remains deterministic and namespace-isolated: `web_receipts` uplift only to `witnessed_web` (never gateway), `sandbox` requires attestation/execution evidence, and VIR canonical selection now uses shared comparator (`compareVirCandidate` + `SOURCE_SCORE`) for global stability. Covered by `test/proof-tier-next-trust.test.ts`, `test/vir-proof-tier.test.ts`, and `test/web-receipt.test.ts`.

userStories.title: Schema-runtime drift gate in CI for clawverify
userStories.description: As an operator, I want CI to fail when schema changes are not mirrored in validators/runtime/tests.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a drift-check script (schema registry + validator exports + generated validators consistency)
CI workflow runs drift check and fails on unsynced schema/runtime state
Validator generation step is enforced (generated file drift detected by CI)
Add at least one test/assertion to confirm new schema refs are wired into runtime validation
Typecheck and CI checks pass
userStories.notes: Added schema/runtime drift gate via `services/clawverify/scripts/check-schema-runtime-sync.mjs` and CI workflow `.github/workflows/clawverify-schema-drift.yml`. The gate verifies schema registry + validator export wiring, enforces regenerated standalone validators, and fails schema-only deltas without clawverify runtime/test updates. Added explicit contract coverage in `test/schema-runtime-contract.test.ts` for `vir_receipts` (VIR v2), `web_receipts`, and `coverage_attestations` schema wiring.

userStories.title: Deterministic firewall conformance fixture corpus
userStories.description: As a platform, I want reproducible fixture-based verification vectors so trust-tier and fail-code regressions are caught immediately.
acceptanceCriteria: Add fixture corpus for VALID tiers (self, witnessed_web, gateway, sandbox/tee_measured)
Add fixture corpus for INVALID critical VIR failures (merkle mismatch, unreported conflict, precedence violation, revoked TEE)
Add conformance runner script that asserts expected status + proof_tier + error code
Wire fixture runner into CI or documented smoke gate
Typecheck and tests pass
userStories.notes: Added deterministic firewall fixture corpus under `packages/schema/fixtures/protocol-conformance/clawverify-firewall/` (valid self/witnessed_web/gateway/sandbox+tee and invalid critical VIR + revoked TEE cases). Added conformance test `services/clawverify/test/firewall-conformance.test.ts` and runner `scripts/protocol/run-clawverify-firewall-conformance.mjs` that asserts expected status/proof_tier/error_code per fixture. Wired into CI via `.github/workflows/clawverify-firewall-conformance.yml`.

userStories.title: TEE attestation operational hardening for fail-closed uplift
userStories.description: As a verifier, I want tee_measured uplift only under strict allowlist + binding + revocation checks.
acceptanceCriteria: Execution attestation verification requires signer allowlist and binding to agent_did/run_id/proof_bundle_hash_b64u
TEE root/TCB allowlist and revocation checks are enforced for tee_execution
Revoked root/TCB deterministically returns INVALID with REVOKED semantics
model_identity_tier can be tee_measured only when tee_execution evidence verifies
Add tests for revoked, mismatch, and valid uplift paths
userStories.notes: TEE attestation path is fail-closed in proof-bundle verification: signer allowlist + agent/run/proof-bundle-hash bindings are enforced, root/TCB allowlist+revocation checks gate `tee_execution`, and revoked claims hard-fail with `REVOKED`. Verified by `test/proof-tier-next-trust.test.ts` (valid uplift + revoked hard-fail) and full clawverify suite pass.

userStories.title: Harness matrix expansion: Gemini CLI lane and Linux-ready verification lane
userStories.description: As a platform, I want the harness matrix to include Gemini CLI and Linux-lane validation so PoH trust claims hold cross-runtime.
acceptanceCriteria: Add Gemini CLI path to harness matrix and e2e runner inputs
Codex wrapper and Gemini lanes both produce verifiable PoH artifacts
Define Linux verification lane script/job for clawverify/clawsig paths with deterministic outputs
Document artifact paths and expected pass/fail signals
Typecheck/tests for modified packages pass
userStories.notes: Expanded harness matrix agent set to include Gemini CLI lane in `packages/clawsig-conformance/src/harness-matrix.ts` (agent enum/default matrix/env wiring). Linux lane is now strict-gated in `.github/workflows/poh-harness-matrix-linux.yml` with deterministic CI shims for required codex+gemini mock lanes. `scripts/e2e/linux-harness-smoke.mjs` emits both `.clawsig/e2e-linux-results.json` and `.clawsig/e2e-linux-strict-summary.json` with explicit failure codes (`AGENT_UNAVAILABLE`, `PARITY_FAILURE`) and blocks CI when required lanes fail.

userStories.title: Coverage attestation runtime binding and enforcement
userStories.description: As a verifier, I want coverage_attestations to be cryptographically verified and bound to the proof bundle run/chain so enforcement phases operate on verified evidence instead of schema presence.
acceptanceCriteria: Add runtime coverage attestation verifier module with schema validation, payload hash recompute, signature verification, and signer allowlist
Verify payload.coverage_attestations against bundle binding (agent_did, run_id, event_chain_root_hash_b64u) in verify-proof-bundle
Expose and populate deterministic coverage component counters (count, signature_verified_count, verified_count, valid)
Enforcement semantics are deterministic: observe/warn add risk flags only; enforce downgrades only on verified coverage invariant failures or explicitly required coverage absence
Wire env options in Worker entrypoint for signer allowlist, enforcement phase, and liveness max gap
Typecheck, tests, schema-drift gate, and firewall conformance pass
userStories.notes: Implemented `src/verify-coverage-attestation.ts` and wired proof-bundle runtime verification for `coverage_attestations` with fail-closed signer allowlist, hash+signature checks, and bundle binding checks (`agent_did`, `run_id`, `event_chain_root_hash_b64u`). Added deterministic counters: `coverage_attestations_count`, `coverage_attestations_signature_verified_count`, `coverage_attestations_verified_count`, `coverage_attestations_valid`. Established phase-safe baseline where `observe`/`warn` are non-blocking risk-flag modes; strict fail-closed enforce behavior is coupled in CVF-US-058 policy wiring. Exposed env passthrough in `src/index.ts`: `COVERAGE_ATTESTATION_SIGNER_DIDS`, `COVERAGE_ENFORCEMENT_PHASE`, `COVERAGE_MAX_LIVENESS_GAP_MS`. Added/updated tests in `test/coverage-attestation.test.ts`, `test/proof-tier-next-trust.test.ts`, and firewall conformance fixtures.

userStories.title: Strict coverage enforcement policy coupling (WPC v2)
userStories.description: As a verifier, I want /v1/verify/bundle coverage phase behavior to be explicitly driven by a validated Work Policy Contract so enforce mode is fail-closed on missing/invalid coverage evidence.
acceptanceCriteria: Add coverage_enforcement enum (observe|warn|enforce, default observe) to work_policy_contract.v2 schema
Validate optional work_policy_contract object in /v1/verify/bundle request using local schema validation (no network fetch)
Derive effective coverage phase from WPC when present, otherwise fall back to COVERAGE_ENFORCEMENT_PHASE env
When effective phase is enforce, missing/invalid required coverage evidence returns INVALID (no downgrade-only behavior)
observe/warn phases remain non-blocking and emit risk flags
Typecheck/tests/schema-drift/firewall-conformance pass
userStories.notes: Updated `packages/schema/policy/work_policy_contract.v2.json` with `coverage_enforcement` and regenerated standalone validators. Added `validateWorkPolicyContractV2` wiring in schema-validation and used it in `src/index.ts` for `/v1/verify/bundle` request-level validation of optional `work_policy_contract`. Effective phase precedence is now deterministic: WPC field when provided, else env fallback. Updated `verify-proof-bundle.ts` enforce semantics to fail closed (`INVALID` + `EVIDENCE_MISMATCH`) when coverage evidence is missing or invalid under enforce. Added API/runtime regression coverage in `test/coverage-attestation.test.ts`, updated phase behavior checks in `test/proof-tier-next-trust.test.ts`, and extended firewall conformance fixtures/tests for coverage-enforce scenarios.

userStories.title: VIR corroboration gating for high-confidence source claims
userStories.description: As a verifier, I want high-confidence VIR claims to require corroboration so trust uplift cannot be granted on uncorroborated assertions.
acceptanceCriteria: High-claim VIR sources are corroboration-gated against allowlisted receipt evidence
Uncorroborated high-claim evidence does not uplift to gateway-equivalent trust
Behavior is deterministic and fail-closed when corroboration invariants fail
Typecheck/tests/schema-runtime-sync/firewall-conformance pass
userStories.notes: Merged via PR #267 (`564608a`) with CVF-US-060. Added deterministic corroboration checks and high-claim demotion rules in `verify-proof-bundle.ts`/`vir-core.ts` with fixture and test coverage.

userStories.title: Deterministic VIR conflict severity policy
userStories.description: As a verifier, I want VIR conflict handling to be severity-driven and deterministic so contradictory claims cannot silently degrade trust semantics.
acceptanceCriteria: Conflict severity policy maps contradictions to deterministic actions
Same-event VIR contradictions fail closed under strict policy
Conflict handling preserves reproducible ordering and outcomes
Typecheck/tests/schema-runtime-sync/firewall-conformance pass
userStories.notes: Merged via PR #267 (`564608a`). Implemented deterministic VIR conflict severity policy, including same-event contradiction fail-closed behavior and conformance fixtures.

userStories.title: Tracker continuity placeholder for CVF-US-061
userStories.description: As maintainers, we need CVF-US-061 explicitly tracked so story numbering and audit history remain continuous and reviewable.
acceptanceCriteria: CVF-US-061 is present in services/clawverify/prd.json with passes:false
Notes explicitly mark implementation status as pending/deferred
Reinstatement lane introduces no runtime or schema behavior changes
userStories.notes: Record reinstated for auditability after numbering-gap discovery. Implementation status is pending/deferred; no runtime change has shipped in this tracker-only lane.

userStories.title: Witnessed-web quorum policy enforcement
userStories.description: As a verifier, I want witnessed-web evidence to require quorum semantics so single-witness artifacts cannot overstate trust.
acceptanceCriteria: m-of-n quorum policy is evaluated per run_id + event_hash binding context
Split-view conflicts are detected deterministically
warn mode degrades witnessed_web uplift; enforce mode returns INVALID
Typecheck/tests/schema-runtime-sync/firewall-conformance pass
userStories.notes: Merged via PR #268 (`e59d25e`). Added witnessed-web quorum m-of-n checks and split-view conflict detection with deterministic warn/enforce policy semantics.

userStories.title: Witnessed-web transparency anchoring hooks
userStories.description: As a verifier, I want optional/strict transparency anchoring hooks for witnessed-web receipts so policy can require log inclusion proofs when needed.
acceptanceCriteria: Transparency policy modes optional|warn|enforce are supported
required_after cutoff is honored deterministically
Receipt leaf binding to transparency proof is verified
Typecheck/tests/schema-runtime-sync/firewall-conformance pass
userStories.notes: Merged via PR #268 (`e59d25e`). Reused `verifyLogInclusionProof(...)` and added deterministic receipt-leaf binding checks plus policy hooks in verify-bundle path.

userStories.title: TEE nonce/report-data binding and deterministic tee tier uplift
userStories.description: As a verifier, I want tee_execution attestations bound to deterministic run/proof-bundle context so replay/mismatch attempts fail closed and tee tiering remains explicit.
acceptanceCriteria: TEE nonce/report-data binding is validated against deterministic context hash
TEE measured identity requires model_weights digest for acceptance
Valid TEE evidence yields explicit proof_tier=tee
tee_execution claim failures (root/TCB/revocation/nonce/binding) are INVALID with no fallback
Typecheck/tests/schema-runtime-sync/firewall-conformance pass
userStories.notes: Merged via PR #269 (`2802d59`). Added additive schema fields for TEE nonce/model-weights, strict runtime requirements, fail-closed tee claim handling, and explicit tee tier semantics across verify-bundle and verify-agent paths.

userStories.title: Deterministic completeness contract for proof bundle verification
userStories.description: As a verifier, I want explicit completeness verdict/reason outputs so partial observability is machine-auditable and non-narrative.
acceptanceCriteria: Introduce typed completeness verdict + reason-code unions in clawverify runtime types
Populate component_results completeness fields on every proof bundle verification path
Completeness semantics are deterministic and independent of probabilistic scoring
Typecheck/tests/schema-runtime-sync/firewall+binary-semantic conformance pass
userStories.notes: Shipped via PR #277 (merge 29913c4) with feature commit 25c8ea984d79b12a0dada47e9a49b31c3e46568a and proof commit f19afc17667ed1478386ca31c43fc5d666f4c8d9.

userStories.title: Runtime completeness evaluator with deterministic fail-closed coupling
userStories.description: As an operator, I want completeness policy evaluation over evidence classes so binding/context contradictions fail closed and incomplete classes constrain tiers deterministically.
acceptanceCriteria: Evaluate event chain, binding context, receipts, and attestations with deterministic precedence
Inconsistent completeness outcomes fail closed with deterministic EVIDENCE_MISMATCH surface
Incomplete completeness outcomes constrain trust/proof tier uplift without probabilistic heuristics
No string-parsed security decisions in completeness policy path
